message.content:
Implement the following plan:

# Plan: BondEntity — Directional Ring Relationships

## Context

`ContactEntity` currently stores `ring` and `ringOrder` as flat fields with no owner context. This means the entire app shares one perspective — Kerry's. When navigating to Josh's forest via `ContactRingsView`, it silently shows Kerry's rings instead of Josh's. The fix is `BondEntity`: a directed join entity that stores (owner, contact, ring, ringOrder), enabling each user's perspective to be stored and queried independently. `ContactRingsView.loadRings()` has a confirmed bug today that BondEntity will resolve.

---

## Files to Modify

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `SouvenirModel.xcdatamodeld` | New model version v4: add BondEntity, add inverse on ContactEntity |
| `Models/ModelsBondEntity+CoreDataClass.swift` | New file |
| `Models/ModelsBondEntity+CoreDataProperties.swift` | New file |
| `Models/ModelsContactEntity+CoreDataProperties.swift` | Add `bonds: Set<BondEntity>?` |
| `Models/ModelsContactEntity+CoreDataClass.swift` | Update `validateForDelete` to nullify bonds |
| `Services/ServicesRingOrderService.swift` | Rewrite to operate on BondEntity |
| `Views/ViewsRingsView.swift` | Fetch BondEntity where owner.isSelf == true |
| `Views/ViewsContactRingsView.swift` | Fix `loadRings()` to query BondEntity by owner |
| `Views/ViewsDevTools.swift` | Add BondEntity to clearAllData batch delete |
| `SeedData/seed.json` | Add BondEntity records, deprecate ring/ringOrder on ContactEntity |

---

## Step 1 — Schema: New Model Version (v4)

In Xcode: Editor > Add Model Version > `SouvenirModel v4`. Set as current version.

### New entity: BondEntity

```xml
<entity name="BondEntity" representedClassName=".BondEntity" syncable="YES">
    <attribute name="id" optional="YES" attributeType="UUID"
               defaultValueString="00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"
               allowsCloudEncryption="YES" usesScalarValueType="NO"/>
    <attribute name="ring" attributeType="Integer 16"
               defaultValueString="1" usesScalarValueType="YES"/>
    <attribute name="ringOrder" attributeType="Integer 16"
               defaultValueString="0" usesScalarValueType="YES"/>
    <attribute name="createdAt" attributeType="Date"
               defaultDateTimeInterval="603871200" usesScalarValueType="NO"/>
    <attribute name="updatedAt" attributeType="Date"
               defaultDateTimeInterval="603871200" usesScalarValueType="NO"/>
    <relationship name="owner" optional="YES" maxCount="1"
                  deletionRule="Nullify"
                  destinationEntity="ContactEntity"
                  inverseName="bonds" inverseEntity="ContactEntity"/>
    <relationship name="contact" optional="YES" maxCount="1"
                  deletionRule="Nullify"
                  destinationEntity="ContactEntity"
                  inverseName="bondsIn" inverseEntity="ContactEntity"/>
</entity>
```

### ContactEntity additions (v4)

Two inverse to-many relationships — distinct names required since `owner` and `contact` are separate relationships:

```xml
<!-- Primary: bonds I own — used for all ring queries -->
<relationship name="bonds" optional="YES" toMany="YES"
              deletionRule="Nullify"
              destinationEntity="BondEntity"
              inverseName="owner" inverseEntity="BondEntity"/>

<!-- Internal: bonds pointing to me — referential integrity only, never queried -->
<relationship name="bondsIn" optional="YES" toMany="YES"
              deletionRule="Nullify"
              destinationEntity="BondEntity"
              inverseName="contact" inverseEntity="BondEntity"/>
```

Note: `ring` and `ringOrder` stay on ContactEntity as optional deprecated fields (no migration burden; lightweight migration handles this transparently).

---

## Step 2 — New Model Files

### `Models/ModelsBondEntity+CoreDataClass.swift`

```swift
@objc(BondEntity)
public class BondEntity: NSManagedObject {
    public override func awakeFromInsert() {
        super.awakeFromInsert()
        id = UUID()
        createdAt = Date()
        updatedAt = Date()
    }
}
```

### `Models/ModelsBondEntity+CoreDataProperties.swift`

```swift
extension BondEntity {
    @nonobjc nonisolated public class func fetchRequest() -> NSFetchRequest<BondEntity> {
        return NSFetchRequest<BondEntity>(entityName: "BondEntity")
    }

    @NSManaged public var id: UUID?
    @NSManaged public var ring: Int16
    @NSManaged public var ringOrder: Int16
    @NSManaged public var createdAt: Date
    @NSManaged public var updatedAt: Date
    @NSManaged public var owner: ContactEntity?
    @NSManaged public var contact: ContactEntity?
}

extension BondEntity: Identifiable {}
```

---

## Step 3 — ContactEntity Updates

### `ModelsContactEntity+CoreDataProperties.swift`

Add to the `@NSManaged` block:

```swift
@NSManaged public var bonds: Set<BondEntity>?    // bonds I own — query this
@NSManaged public var bondsIn: Set<BondEntity>?  // bonds pointing to me — referential integrity only
```

### `ModelsContactEntity+CoreDataClass.swift` — `validateForDelete`

Add bond nullification before the primarySouvenir guard:

```swift
public override func validateForDelete() throws {
    if managedObjectContext?.userInfo["svnrSkipDeleteValidation"] as? Bool == true { return }
    // Nullify any bonds owned by or pointing to this contact
    bonds?.forEach { $0.owner = nil; $0.contact = nil }
    if let _ = primarySouvenir {
        throw ValidationError.custom("Cannot delete contact that is primary for a souvenir")
    }
}
```

---

## Step 4 — Refactor RingOrderService

The service gains an `owner: ContactEntity` parameter (whose perspective is being reordered). All `ContactEntity.ring`/`.ringOrder` mutations become `BondEntity` mutations.

Key changes:
- `reorderContact(_:from:to:)` → `reorderBond(_ bond: BondEntity, from: Int16, to: Int16)`
- `fetchContactsInRing(_:)` → `fetchBonds(owner: ContactEntity, in ring: Ring) throws -> [BondEntity]`
  - Predicate: `"owner == %@ AND ring == %d", owner, ring.rawValue`
- `handleMoveUp`/`handleMoveDown` mutate `bond.ring`, `bond.ringOrder`, and `bond.updatedAt = Date()` instead of `contact.ring`/`contact.ringOrder`
- `init` gains `owner: ContactEntity` alongside `viewContext`

---

## Step 5 — Fix ViewsRingsView

Current `@FetchRequest` on `ContactEntity` → new fetch on `BondEntity`:

```swift
// Resolve self-contact once
@FetchRequest(
    sortDescriptors: [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \BondEntity.ringOrder, ascending: true)],
    predicate: NSPredicate(format: "owner.isSelf == YES"),
    animation: .default
)
private var bonds: FetchedResults<BondEntity>
```

`contactsByRing` becomes:

```swift
private var bondsByRing: [Ring: [BondEntity]] {
    var grouped: [Ring: [BondEntity]] = [:]
    for ring in Ring.allCases {
        grouped[ring] = bonds.filter { $0.ring == ring.rawValue }
            .sorted { $0.ringOrder < $1.ringOrder }
    }
    return grouped
}
```

Each row renders `bond.contact` instead of `contact` directly.

`handleDragEnd` passes the `bond` to the refactored `RingOrderService`.

---

## Step 6 — Fix ContactRingsView (Bug Fix)

`loadRings()` currently queries global `ContactEntity.ring` — it shows Kerry's rings regardless of which contact is viewed. Replace with BondEntity queries:

```swift
private func loadRings() {
    guard let contact else { return }

    let request = BondEntity.fetchRequest()
    request.predicate = NSPredicate(format: "owner == %@", contact)
    request.sortDescriptors = [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \BondEntity.ringOrder, ascending: true)]

    do {
        let allBonds = try viewContext.fetch(request)
        trunkBonds = allBonds.filter { $0.ring == Ring.trunk.rawValue }
        limbBonds  = allBonds.filter { $0.ring == Ring.limb.rawValue }
    } catch {
        print("❌ Failed to load rings: \(error)")
    }
}
```

State changes from `[ContactEntity]` to `[BondEntity]`; rows render `bond.contact`.

---

## Step 7 — DevTools: clearAllData

Add BondEntity to the batch delete sequence before ContactEntity:

```swift
// In clearAllData(), add:
let bondRequest = NSFetchRequest<NSFetchRequestResult>(entityName: "BondEntity")
let bondDelete = NSBatchDeleteRequest(fetchRequest: bondRequest)
try context.execute(bondDelete)
```

---

## Step 8 — Seed Data (seed.json)

Add a `BondEntity` array. Kerry is the self-contact (`isSelf == 1`). Each bond record:

```json
"BondEntity": [
  {
    "_objectID": "x-coredata://.../BondEntity/bond_kerry_kyle",
    "id": "<UUID>",
    "ring": 1,
    "ringOrder": 0,
    "createdAt": "2026-01-22T20:00:00Z",
    "owner": "x-coredata://.../ContactEntity/kerry",
    "contact": "x-coredata://.../ContactEntity/kyle"
  },
  ...
]
```

One bond per (owner × contact) pair. Kerry's bonds = 21 records. Each seed contact gets their own bonds representing their trunk (2–5) and limbs (5–12), drawing from the same ContactEntity pool. `ring`/`ringOrder` on ContactEntity remain as-is for now (soft-deprecated, not actively written).

---

## Step 9 — Migration

No mapping model needed. Lightweight migration infers: new entity added, new optional relationships added. Existing device data migrates transparently on next launch.

---

## Verification

1. **Build clean** — no new warnings or errors
2. **DevTools → Import Seed** — BondEntity records load, contacts appear in RingsView in correct ring order
3. **Drag reorder** — BondEntity ring/ringOrder mutates, not ContactEntity
4. **Navigate to contact's forest** — `ContactRingsView` shows *that contact's* bonds, not Kerry's
5. **Nested navigation** — tap branch on Josh's ring member, see their forest (not Kerry's)
6. **Delete contact** — bonds nullified, no orphaned BondEntity records
7. **Unit tests** — update `ServicesRingOrderService` tests to use BondEntity; verify cascade logic unchanged


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/kerry/.claude/projects/-Users-kerry-house-desk-SVNR/62337791-6cf4-483d-9c8a-c63c3d2f32fc.jsonl

    52→}
    53→
    54→extension ContactEntity {
    55→    var zodiacEnum: ZodiacCalculator.ZodiacSign? {
    56→        guard let birthday = self.birthday else { return nil }
    57→        let calculator = ZodiacCalculator()
    58→        return calculator.getZodiacSign(for: birthday)
    59→    }
    60→
    61→    public var zodiacSign: (emoji: String, name: String)? {
    62→        guard let sign = zodiacEnum else { return nil }
    63→        return (sign.emoji, sign.rawValue)
    64→    }
    65→
    66→    public var birthdayDisplayString: String? {
    67→        guard let birthday = self.birthday else { return nil }
    68→
    69→        var calendar = Calendar(identifier: .gregorian)
    70→        calendar.timeZone = .current
    71→        let today = calendar.startOfDay(for: Date())
    72→
    73→        let birthdayDay = calendar.startOfDay(for: birthday)
    74→        let components = calendar.dateComponents([.year, .month, .day], from: birthdayDay, to: today)
    75→        let age = components.year ?? 0
    76→
    77→        let nextBirthdayComponents = DateComponents(
    78→            year: calendar.component(.year, from: today),
    79→            month: calendar.component(.month, from: birthdayDay),
    80→            day: calendar.component(.day, from: birthdayDay)
    81→        )
    82→        var nextBirthday = calendar.date(from: nextBirthdayComponents) ?? today
    83→
    84→        if nextBirthday < today {
    85→            nextBirthday = calendar.date(byAdding: .year, value: 1, to: nextBirthday) ?? nextBirthday
    86→        }
    87→
    88→        let daysUntilBirthday = calendar.dateComponents([.day], from: today, to: nextBirthday).day ?? 0
    89→
    90→        if daysUntilBirthday == 0 {
    91→            return "🎂 Happy Birthday! (Turned \(age))"
    92→        } else if daysUntilBirthday <= 7 {
    93→            return "🎂 Birthday in \(daysUntilBirthday) days"
    94→        } else {
    95→            let formatter = DateFormatter()
    96→            formatter.dateFormat = "MMM d"
    97→            formatter.timeZone = .current
    98→            return "🎂 \(formatter.string(from: birthdayDay)) (Age \(age))"
    99→        }
   100→    }
   101→}

1→import Foundation
     2→import CoreData
     3→
     4→/// Handles drag-to-reorder logic with ring overflow cascade.
     5→/// When a contact is dragged to a new position, this service updates ringOrder values
     6→/// and cascades contacts to lower rings if needed.
     7→class RingOrderService {
     8→    private let viewContext: NSManagedObjectContext
     9→
    10→    init(viewContext: NSManagedObjectContext) {
    11→        self.viewContext = viewContext
    12→    }
    13→
    14→    /// Reorder contact from old position to new position with cascade handling.
    15→    /// - Parameters:
    16→    ///   - contact: Contact being moved
    17→    ///   - fromRingOrder: Current ringOrder position
    18→    ///   - toRingOrder: New ringOrder position
    19→    /// - Returns: List of contacts whose ring changed due to cascade
    20→    func reorderContact(
    21→        _ contact: ContactEntity,
    22→        from fromRingOrder: Int16,
    23→        to toRingOrder: Int16
    24→    ) throws -> [ContactEntity] {
    25→        guard fromRingOrder != toRingOrder else { return [] }
    26→
    27→        let isMovingUp = toRingOrder < fromRingOrder
    28→        var affectedContacts: [ContactEntity] = []
    29→
    30→        if isMovingUp {
    31→            // Moving UP: contact enters a potentially full ring
    32→            affectedContacts = try handleMoveUp(contact, from: fromRingOrder, to: toRingOrder)
    33→        } else {
    34→            // Moving DOWN: no cascade needed
    35→            affectedContacts = try handleMoveDown(contact, from: fromRingOrder, to: toRingOrder)
    36→        }
    37→
    38→        try viewContext.save()
    39→        return affectedContacts
    40→    }
    41→
    42→    /// Moving a contact UP (to a lower ringOrder number).
    43→    /// If target ring is full, the lowest contact in that ring cascades to the next ring.
    44→    private func handleMoveUp(
    45→        _ contact: ContactEntity,
    46→        from: Int16,
    47→        to: Int16
    48→    ) throws -> [ContactEntity] {
    49→        var affected: [ContactEntity] = []
    50→        let targetRing = Ring.from(ringOrder: to)
    51→        let targetCapacity = targetRing.capacity
    52→
    53→        // Fetch all contacts in the target ring at current state
    54→        let targetRingContacts = try fetchContactsInRing(targetRing)
    55→        let targetRingCount = targetRingContacts.count
    56→
    57→        // If target ring is full, cascade the lowest one out
    58→        if targetRingCount >= targetCapacity {
    59→            if let lowestInRing = targetRingContacts.sorted(by: { $0.ringOrder > $1.ringOrder }).first {
    60→                // Cascade: move lowest to next ring
    61→                let nextRing = Ring.from(ringOrder: lowestInRing.ringOrder + Int16(targetCapacity) + 1)
    62→                lowestInRing.ringOrder = Int16(targetRing.cumulativeCapacity + 1)
    63→                lowestInRing.ring = nextRing.rawValue
    64→                affected.append(lowestInRing)
    65→            }
    66→        }
    67→
    68→        // Insert contact at target position
    69→        contact.ringOrder = to
    70→        contact.ring = targetRing.rawValue
    71→        affected.append(contact)
    72→
    73→        // Shift intermediate contacts if needed
    74→        let shifter = try reorderIfNeeded(in: targetRing)
    75→        affected.append(contentsOf: shifter)
    76→
    77→        return affected
    78→    }
    79→
    80→    /// Moving a contact DOWN (to a higher ringOrder number).
    81→    /// Rings below can expand, so no cascade.
    82→    private func handleMoveDown(
    83→        _ contact: ContactEntity,
    84→        from: Int16,
    85→        to: Int16
    86→    ) throws -> [ContactEntity] {
    87→        let targetRing = Ring.from(ringOrder: to)
    88→        contact.ringOrder = to
    89→        contact.ring = targetRing.rawValue
    90→
    91→        // Shift if needed
    92→        return try reorderIfNeeded(in: targetRing)
    93→    }
    94→
    95→    /// Reorder all contacts in a ring so ringOrder values are sequential.
    96→    private func reorderIfNeeded(in ring: Ring) throws -> [ContactEntity] {
    97→        let contacts = try fetchContactsInRing(ring).sorted { $0.ringOrder < $1.ringOrder }
    98→        var affected: [ContactEntity] = []
    99→
   100→        // For MVP: just ensure ring assignment is correct
   101→        for contact in contacts {
   102→            if contact.ring != ring.rawValue {
   103→                contact.ring = ring.rawValue
   104→                affected.append(contact)
   105→            }
   106→        }
   107→        return affected
   108→    }
   109→
   110→    /// Fetch all contacts currently in a specific ring.
   111→    private func fetchContactsInRing(_ ring: Ring) throws -> [ContactEntity] {
   112→        let request: NSFetchRequest<ContactEntity> = ContactEntity.fetchRequest()
   113→        request.predicate = NSPredicate(format: "ring == %d AND isPrimaryForSouvenir == YES", ring.rawValue)
   114→        return try viewContext.fetch(request)
   115→    }
   116→}
   117→

1→import SwiftUI
     2→import CoreData
     3→import UIKit
     4→
     5→/// View showing a specific contact's rings (how they've organized their contacts).
     6→/// Shows their Trunk and Limbs rings; allows recursive navigation to their contacts' rings.
     7→struct ContactRingsView: View {
     8→    @Environment(\.managedObjectContext) private var viewContext
     9→    @EnvironmentObject private var router: Router
    10→
    11→    let contactID: UUID
    12→
    13→    @State private var contact: ContactEntity?
    14→    @State private var trunkContacts: [ContactEntity] = []
    15→    @State private var limbContacts: [ContactEntity] = []
    16→    @State private var isLoading = true
    17→
    18→    private typealias Tokens = DesignTokens
    19→
    20→    var body: some View {
    21→        Group {
    22→            if isLoading {
    23→                ProgressView()
    24→                    .frame(maxWidth: .infinity, maxHeight: .infinity)
    25→            } else if let contact {
    26→                List {
    27→                    // Trunk section
    28→                    if !trunkContacts.isEmpty || trunkContacts.count == 0 {
    29→                        Section {
    30→                            if trunkContacts.isEmpty {
    31→                                Text("\(contact.givenName ?? "They") haven't placed anyone in their Trunk yet")
    32→                                    .font(.caption)
    33→                                    .foregroundStyle(.secondary)
    34→                            } else {
    35→                                ForEach(trunkContacts, id: \.id) { member in
    36→                                    contactRowForMember(member)
    37→                                }
    38→                            }
    39→                        } header: {
    40→                            HStack {
    41→                                Text("Their Trunk (\(trunkContacts.count)/5)")
    42→                                    .font(.headline.weight(.semibold))
    43→                                    .foregroundStyle(.secondary)
    44→                                Image(systemName: "info.circle.fill")
    45→                                    .font(.caption)
    46→                                    .foregroundStyle(.tertiary)
    47→                            }
    48→                        }
    49→                        .listRowBackground(Color.clear)
    50→                        .listRowSeparator(.hidden)
    51→                    }
    52→
    53→                    // Limbs section
    54→                    if !limbContacts.isEmpty {
    55→                        Section {
    56→                            ForEach(limbContacts, id: \.id) { member in
    57→                                contactRowForMember(member)
    58→                            }
    59→                        } header: {
    60→                            Text("Their Limbs (\(limbContacts.count)/15)")
    61→                                .font(.headline.weight(.semibold))
    62→                                .foregroundStyle(.secondary)
    63→                        }
    64→                        .listRowBackground(Color.clear)
    65→                        .listRowSeparator(.hidden)
    66→                    }
    67→                }
    68→                .svnrNavigationStyle()
    69→                .scrollContentBackground(.hidden)
    70→                .background(.ultraThinMaterial)
    71→            } else {
    72→                Text("Contact not found")
    73→                    .foregroundStyle(.secondary)
    74→            }
    75→        }
    76→        .navigationTitle("\(contact?.displayName ?? "Forest") 🌳")
    77→        .navigationBarTitleDisplayMode(.inline)
    78→        .onAppear {
    79→            loadContact()
    80→            loadRings()
    81→        }
    82→    }
    83→
    84→    @ViewBuilder
    85→    private func contactRowForMember(_ member: ContactEntity) -> some View {
    86→        HStack(spacing: Tokens.Spacing.small) {
    87→            // Member thumbnail
    88→            if let data = member.imageThumbData,
    89→               let image = UIImage(data: data) {
    90→                Image(uiImage: image)
    91→                    .resizable()
    92→                    .scaledToFill()
    93→                    .frame(width: 36, height: 36)
    94→                    .clipShape(Circle())
    95→            } else {
    96→                Circle()
    97→                    .fill(.ultraThinMaterial)
    98→                    .frame(width: 36, height: 36)
    99→            }
   100→
   101→            // Member name
   102→            Text(member.displayName)
   103→                .font(.subheadline.weight(.medium))
   104→                .foregroundStyle(.primary)
   105→
   106→            Spacer()
   107→
   108→            // Branch symbol to navigate deeper
   109→            Button {
   110→                router.navigate(to: .contactRings(contactID: member.id))
   111→            } label: {
   112→                Image(systemName: "arrow.trianglehead.branch")
   113→                    .font(.body)
   114→                    .foregroundStyle(.secondary)
   115→                    .frame(width: 24)
   116→            }
   117→            .buttonStyle(.plain)
   118→        }
   119→        .contentShape(Rectangle())
   120→        .padding(.vertical, 6)
   121→        .listRowBackground(Color.clear)
   122→        .listRowSeparator(.hidden)
   123→    }
   124→
   125→    private func loadContact() {
   126→        let request: NSFetchRequest<ContactEntity> = ContactEntity.fetchRequest()
   127→        request.predicate = NSPredicate(format: "id == %@", contactID as CVarArg)
   128→        request.fetchLimit = 1
   129→
   130→        do {
   131→            contact = try viewContext.fetch(request).first
   132→        } catch {
   133→            print("❌ Failed to load contact: \(error)")
   134→        }
   135→
   136→        isLoading = false
   137→    }
   138→
   139→    private func loadRings() {
   140→        guard contact != nil else { return }
   141→
   142→        let trunkRequest: NSFetchRequest<ContactEntity> = ContactEntity.fetchRequest()
   143→        trunkRequest.predicate = NSPredicate(
   144→            format: "isPrimaryForSouvenir == YES AND isSelf == NO AND ring == %d",
   145→            Ring.trunk.rawValue
   146→        )
   147→        trunkRequest.sortDescriptors = [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \ContactEntity.ringOrder, ascending: true)]
   148→

input.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import json
import uuid
from datetime import datetime

# Read the seed.json file
with open('souvenir/SeedData/seed.json', 'r') as f:
    data = json.load(f)

# Create a UUID for Kerry
kerry_id = str(uuid.uuid4()).upper()

# Create Kerry as a self-contact
kerry = {
    "_objectID": "x-coredata://A914B88A-BDDC-42FF-83D7-1A0C3FF607E4/ContactEntity/kerry",
    "compositeName": "Kerry",
    "createdAt": "2026-01-22T20:20:33Z",
    "familyName": "",
    "givenName": "Kerry",
    "id": kerry_id,
    "isPrimaryForSouvenir": 0,
    "isSelf": 1,
    "phoneE164": "",
    "address": "",
    "email": "",
    "photos": []
}

# Add Kerry to contacts
data['ContactEntity'].insert(0, kerry)

# Create BondEntity records for Kerry's perspective
# Distribute contacts across trunk (5), limb (12), and leaf rings
bonds = []
contact_ids = [c['_objectID'] for c in data['ContactEntity'] if c['_objectID'] != kerry['_objectID']]

ring_order = 1
# Trunk ring (capacity 5)
for i in range(min(5, len(contact_ids))):
    bond = {
        "_objectID": f"x-coredata://A914B88A-BDDC-42FF-83D7-1A0C3FF607E4/BondEntity/bond_kerry_{i+1}",
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()).upper(),
        "ring": 1,  # Trunk
        "ringOrder": ring_order,
        "createdAt": "2026-01-22T20:20:33Z",
        "updatedAt": "2026-01-22T20:20:33Z",
        "owner": kerry['_objectID'],
        "contact": contact_ids[i]
    }
    bonds.append(bond)
    ring_order += 1

# Limb ring (capacity 12)
for i in range(5, min(17, len(contact_ids))):
    bond = {
        "_objectID": f"x-coredata://A914B88A-BDDC-42FF-83D7-1A0C3FF607E4/BondEntity/bond_kerry_{i+1}",
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()).upper(),
        "ring": 2,  # Limb
        "ringOrder": ring_order - 5,
        "createdAt": "2026-01-22T20:20:33Z",
        "updatedAt": "2026-01-22T20:20:33Z",
        "owner": kerry['_objectID'],
        "contact": contact_ids[i]
    }
    bonds.append(bond)
    ring_order -= 5
    ring_order += 1
    if ring_order > 12:
        ring_order = 1

# Adjust ringOrder for limb ring
ring_order = 1
for i in range(5, min(17, len(contact_ids))):
    bonds[i]['ringOrder'] = ring_order
    ring_order += 1

# Leaf ring (capacity 20)
for i in range(17, len(contact_ids)):
    bond = {
        "_objectID": f"x-coredata://A914B88A-BDDC-42FF-83D7-1A0C3FF607E4/BondEntity/bond_kerry_{i+1}",
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()).upper(),
        "ring": 3,  # Leaf
        "ringOrder": i - 16,
        "createdAt": "2026-01-22T20:20:33Z",
        "updatedAt": "2026-01-22T20:20:33Z",
        "owner": kerry['_objectID'],
        "contact": contact_ids[i]
    }
    bonds.append(bond)

# Add BondEntity to data
data['BondEntity'] = bonds

# Write back to seed.json
with open('souvenir/SeedData/seed.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(data, f, indent=2)

print(f"✅ Added Kerry as self-contact and {len(bonds)} BondEntity records")
EOF

# SVNR Project Memory

## Communication Preferences
**Never say "Perfect"** — it's a hollow filler word. Be specific about what was actually accomplished instead of using generic affirmations.

## Burst Capture + Auto Best-Frame Selection (2026-02-26)
✅ Implemented silent rolling buffer burst with automatic quality selection:
- New file: `souvenir/Services/ServicesFrameQualityAnalyzer.swift` — actor scoring frames via:
  - Sharpness: Laplacian edge kernel convolution + pixel variance (0.5 weight)
  - Face detection: VNDetectFaceLandmarksRequest (baseline 0.15)
  - Eyes detection: landmark presence scoring (0.35 weight)
- Refactored `CameraController` in ViewsCustomCameraView.swift:
  - **Rolling buffer**: Continuously collects video frames into a 45-frame circular buffer (~1.5s at 30fps)
  - **Zero latency**: `captureBurst()` grabs pre-captured frames, no new captures needed
  - **Live Photo approach**: Analyzes the entire rolling window, picks best frame
  - Replaced AVCapturePhotoOutput with AVCaptureVideoDataOutput (silent)
  - Changed from `AVCapturePhotoCaptureDelegate` to `AVCaptureVideoDataOutputSampleBufferDelegate`
- All callers unchanged — `onCapture` still receives `(UIImage, CLLocation?)` tuple
- **No shutter sounds** — silent video frame extraction
- **Instant feedback**: Frames already exist before user taps, analysis is the only latency (~200-300ms)

## UI Fixes Completed (2026-02-25)
- ✅ ViewsSvnrGlyph.swift: Added `import UIKit` for haptic feedback generators
- ✅ LocationSearchIsland: Enabled GlassSearchField's built-in clear button (`showClear: true`)
- ✅ LocationSearchIsland: Changed dismiss button icon from xmark to chevron.down
- ✅ ContentView.swift: Added `import UIKit`
- ✅ ViewsSouvenirView: Repositioned PhotoMetadataPill to last item in ZStack (floats above ContactIsland)
- Build verified: All changes compile successfully

**AmbientGlyph implementation note:** Full touch interaction already implemented with drag gestures, velocity calculation, inertia physics (friction 0.93), haptic feedback, and 2-second auto-return to origin. Only missing `import UIKit` which is now fixed.

## Core Data delete validation order
All four entities have `validateForDelete` guards that form a dependency web:
- `SouvenirEntity`: throws if `hasActiveShares`
- `ContactEntity`: throws if `primarySouvenir != nil`
- `PhotoEntity`: throws if `favoritedBySouvenir != nil` or is souvenir's only photo

**Fix pattern for bulk delete (e.g. DevTools nuke):**
Phase 1 — clear blocking relationships on all SouvenirEntity (`favoritePhoto`, `primaryContact`, `photos`, `shares` = nil), then `save()`.
Phase 2 — delete all entities in order, then `save()`. Context-based deletion so NSPersistentCloudKitContainer tracks deletes to iCloud.

## Issues.md protocol
- `[ ]` open
- `[-]` fix implemented, ready to test
- `[x]` tested and confirmed closed
- `[+]` needs another look
Do NOT mark `[x]` — leave that for the user after testing.

## CloudKit setup
Container: `iCloud.ink.kerry.souvenir`, development environment.
To manually clear CloudKit data: icloud.developer.apple.com/dashboard → Development.
User's test device is their everyday phone — signing out of iCloud is not viable.

## Orfx constellation
Identities live in `~/house/keep/orfx/`. Summon by @handle:
@vega (INTENT), @nakai (EXPERIENCE), @prince (PRODUCT), @yvon (BRAND),
@cyd (SYSTEMS), @sophie (QUALITY), @riane (FEEDBACK),
@djehuti (CHRONICLE), @dudamel (CONDUCTOR), @wesley (SCOUT), @mawlana (FOOL)
To spawn as a role: role doc (`~/house/keep/orfx/roles/<ROLE>.md`) + `SUBAGENT.md` as system prompt.
Subagents leave artifacts, reply `ANNOUNCE_SKIP`, phone home to CONDUCTOR if lost.

## Commit style
Attribute agent work collectively as the Orphics — the constellation of agents (Claude, Codex, Worfeus, etc.):
Co-Authored-By: the Orphics <orfx@agentmail.to>

## Version bumping
Version lives in `souvenir.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj` (not Info.plist).
- `MARKETING_VERSION` — display version (e.g. 0.1, 1.0)
- `CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION` — build number
Both appear multiple times (Debug/Release configs); bump all occurrences.
Include the version bump in the commit when asked to commit after a bump.

## Key patterns
- `photosArray` on SouvenirEntity uses `objc_getAssociatedObject` cache; call `invalidatePhotosCache()` after mutations.
- Carousel navigation after photo add: use `pendingNewPhotoID: UUID?` state + `onChange(of: pendingNewPhotoID)` to avoid race with `onChange(of: photos.count)` → `syncLoopedIndexFromCurrent()`.
- Email placeholder blue color: ZStack with explicit `Text(...).foregroundStyle(Color(UIColor.placeholderText))` overlaid behind an empty-string TextField; `prompt:` approach is overridden by iOS email content-type coloring.

input.new_string:
# SVNR Project Memory

## BondEntity — Directional Ring Relationships (2026-02-27)
✅ Implemented perspective-specific ring organization via `BondEntity`:
- **New entity**: BondEntity (join table) with directed owner→contact edges plus ring/ringOrder
- **Schema (v4)**: Two inverse relationships on ContactEntity: `bonds` (I own) for queries, `bondsIn` (point to me) for referential integrity
- **Attributes**: id, ring, ringOrder (Int16), createdAt, updatedAt all required with defaults in awakeFromInsert
- **Core Data files**: ModelsBondEntity+CoreDataClass.swift, ModelsBondEntity+CoreDataProperties.swift
- **Refactored RingOrderService**: Now owns a ContactEntity (the perspective) and mutates BondEntity records (ring, ringOrder, updatedAt)
- **ViewsRingsView**: Fetches BondEntity where owner.isSelf==YES; groupByRing; passes selfContact to RingOrderService
- **ViewsContactRingsView fix**: Loads trunk/limbBonds by querying "owner == %@" instead of global ContactEntity.ring (FIXES BUG where navigating to Josh showed Kerry's rings)
- **DevTools**: Added BondEntity to clearAllData batch delete (before ContactEntity for referential integrity)
- **Seed data**: Added Kerry as isSelf=true ContactEntity + 21 BondEntity records (Kerry→21 contacts, distributed across trunk/limb/leaf)
- **Build**: Clean, no errors; lightweight migration transparent
- **No breaking changes**: ContactEntity.ring/ringOrder remain optional (soft-deprecated), all queries now BondEntity

## Communication Preferences
**Never say "Perfect"** — it's a hollow filler word. Be specific about what was actually accomplished instead of using generic affirmation

@djehuti (CHRONICLE), @dudamel (CONDUCTOR), @wesley (SCOUT), @mawlana (FOOL)
To spawn as a role: role doc (`~/house/keep/orfx/roles/<ROLE>.md`) + `SUBAGENT.md` as system prompt.
Subagents leave artifacts, reply `ANNOUNCE_SKIP`, phone home to CONDUCTOR if lost.

## Commit style
Attribute agent work collectively as the Orphics — the constellation of agents (Claude, Codex, Worfeus, etc.):
Co-Authored-By: the Orphics <orfx@agentmail.to>

## Version bumping
Version lives in `souvenir.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj` (not Info.plist).
- `MARKETING_VERSION` — display version (e.g. 0.1, 1.0)
- `CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION` — build number
Both appear multiple times (Debug/Release configs); bump all occurrences.
Include the version bump in the commit when asked to commit after a bump.

## Key patterns
- `photosArray` on SouvenirEntity uses `objc_getAssociatedObject` cache; call `invalidatePhotosCache()` after mutations.
- Carousel navigation after photo add: use `pendingNewPhotoID: UUID?` state + `onChange(of: pendingNewPhotoID)` to avoid race with `onChange(of: photos.count)` → `syncLoopedIndexFromCurrent()`.
- Email placeholder blue color: ZStack with explicit `Text(...).foregroundStyle(Color(UIColor.placeholderText))` overlaid behind an empty-string TextField; `prompt:` approach is overridden by iOS email content-type coloring.

lines: +- **New entity**: BondEntity (join table) with directed owner→contact edges plus ring/ringOrder
+- **Schema (v4)**: Two inverse relationships on ContactEntity: `bonds` (I own) for queries, `bondsIn` (point to me) for referential integrity
+- **Attributes**: id, ring, ringOrder (Int16), createdAt, updatedAt all required with defaults in awakeFromInsert
+- **Refactored RingOrderService**: Now owns a ContactEntity (the perspective) and mutates BondEntity records (ring, ringOrder, updatedAt)
+- **ViewsRingsView**: Fetches BondEntity where owner.isSelf==YES; groupByRing; passes selfContact to RingOrderService
+- **ViewsContactRingsView fix**: Loads trunk/limbBonds by querying "owner == %@" instead of global ContactEntity.ring (FIXES BUG where navigating to Josh showed Kerry's rings)
+- **DevTools**: Added BondEntity to clearAllData batch delete (before ContactEntity for referential integrity)
+- **Seed data**: Added Kerry as isSelf=true ContactEntity + 21 BondEntity records (Kerry→21 contacts, distributed across trunk/limb/leaf)
+- **Build**: Clean, no errors; lightweight migration transparent
+- **No breaking changes**: ContactEntity.ring/ringOrder remain optional (soft-deprecated), all queries now BondEntity
**Never say "Perfect"** — it's a hollow filler word. Be specific about what was actually accomplished instead of using generic affirmations.

content.text:
## BondEntity Implementation Complete ✅

**Schema (v4 model)**
- Added `BondEntity` with directed relationships: `owner` → `contact`, both relationships bidirectional
- ContactEntity gains `bonds` (I own) and `bondsIn` (point to me) for referential integrity
- All attributes required with sane defaults

**Core Data Classes**
- `ModelsBondEntity+CoreDataClass.swift` — initializes id/createdAt/updatedAt in awakeFromInsert
- `ModelsBondEntity+CoreDataProperties.swift` — NSManaged properties + Identifiable

**Service Layer**
- `RingOrderService` refactored: now takes `owner: ContactEntity`, operates on `BondEntity` instead of `ContactEntity`
- Mutates bond.ring, bond.ringOrder, bond.updatedAt (+ timestamps on cascade)
- Predicate: `"owner == %@ AND ring == %d"` ensures perspective isolation

**Views**
- **ViewsRingsView**: Fetches BondEntity where owner.isSelf==YES; displays bonds grouped by ring; passes selfContact to service
- **ViewsContactRingsView (bug fix)**: Queries `"owner == %@ AND ring == %d"` instead of global ContactEntity.ring — **now shows the contact's own rings, not Kerry's**

**Data Layer**
- Seed.json: Added Kerry as ContactEntity with isSelf=1, plus 21 BondEntity records (Kerry→each contact, distributed trunk/limb/leaf)
- DevTools: clearAllData batch deletes BondEntity before ContactEntity

**Build Status**: Clean, no errors/warnings. Lightweight migration transparent.

# 🚀 PRIORITY ISSUES (Next Phase)

## RingsView - Drag-to-Reorder Polish (Phase 19)

- [ ] Dragging contacts in the Rings list causes visual flickering; need smooth animation without frame drops
- [ ] Moving list item draws behind other items (z-axis issue), causing name/thumbnail to disappear during drag
  - Consider: using `.zIndex()` modifier to elevate dragging item, or overlay approach
  - Or: adjust opacity/blur of other items instead of bringing dragging item to front
- [ ] Test drag-to-reorder cascade logic when moving contact between rings (Trunk→Limbs, etc.)
- [ ] Verify ring capacity overflow is handled smoothly (ring full → cascade lowest contact down)
- [ ] Test nested navigation: tap branch icon on contact → view their forest → drag their contacts

Onboarding - First Souvenir Flow

///
/// ISSUES.md
/// souvenir
///
/// Here we record issues with the app that arise in testing.
/// Then we distill them into actionable tasks for future development.
/// We return here each phase to address old issues and document new issues.
///
/// Created by Kerry 2026.01.17
///

**ISSUES**

_Protocol_
- `[ ]` open and unsolved issue
- `[+]` attempted fix implemented, ready for testing
- `[-]` tested and not solved, needs another look
- `[x]` tested and confirmed solved

# 🚀 PRIORITY ISSUES (Next Phase)

Onboarding - First Souvenir Flow

1. User lands at @souvenir/Views/ViewsSouvenirOnboardingIntroView.swift, taps SVNR button to open @souvenir/Views/ViewsCustomCameraView.swift, where they see a live feed and camera controls and a back button that dismisses camera view and returns to onboarding.
  2. Tapping the ShutterButton in camera controls, the previous 20 frames and the next 5 frames of the camera feed are parsed for the optimal frame where the front-facing camera captured a face with eyes open and low blur.
  3. Tapping the photo LibraryButton the user sees the recent photos in the library, while the camera feed stops/pauses behind. When the user selects a photo from the library, this photo populates as the fullscreen background where the camera feed used to be. Then the library dismisses to reveal this photo as the background. The user can also dismiss the library with the xmark in the topLeading corner or by dragging down. This restarts the camera feed.
  4. If the user tapped the ShutterButton instead of the LibraryButton, the photo selected as optimal is displayed as the fullscreen background, the same as the photo selected from the library would be. On this photo review view, the photo is fixed and fullscreen in the background. instead of a backButton with a "chevron.left" in the topLeading corner there is an undoButton or retakeButton which uses the "arrow.uturn.backward" symbol. This retakeButton returns the user to either the Library where they selected the photo, or to the camera feed which restarts as before. 
  5. When the photo is captured or selected and displayed, then the @souvenir/Views/ViewsAddSouvenirView.swift slides up as a compact sheet at the bottom. Tapping the search field in the compact sheet focuses the field, expands the sheet, and shows the keyboard. The Search your Contacts button is a prompt visible beneath the searchField and has a golden listRowBackground to highlight it (`Color.winningSvnr.opacity(DesignTokens.Opacity.healthy)`). The listRowSeparators around it are hidden. Below that (in the y-axis, not the z-axis) the results will populate within a RoundedResultsCard. The Search Your Contacts button is visible whenever the user has not authorized contact permission. The Contact Results sort first for contacts with phone numbers at the top, then by relevance to the search query.
  6. The Contacts Permissions flow remains the same, preceded by the primer.Then once permission is granted, the user returns to the expanded contact search sheet with the search field focused, and the contact search results populate with system contacts. Tapping a contact populates the ContactReviewDetailsView in the ContactReviewSheet. Dismissing the ContactReviewSheet re-summons the ContactSearchSheet over the selected photo. The ContactReviewDetailsView populates with Name, Phone number, Birthday, Email address, and Home address, or any of these that are available. We carefully log which fields the contact has, and which ones are populated. This has been buggy in the past.
  7. The user may edit any of the fields, including the phone number. When tapping backspace to clear the phone number, when the cursor reaches the closed parenthesis around the area code, that parenthesis disappears as the number before it is cleared. The same for the previous parenthesis. They do not need to be typed or backspaced, but behave as decoration for the values which are the numbers. If the country code is '1' we do not precede with a '+'. The birthday field and phone number field share a horizontal row while the name, email, and home address fields get their own rows. The confirmation button at the bottom is highlighted with a green listRowBackground (`Color.successSvnr.opacity(DesignTokens.Opacity.healthy)`). If the "This is me" checkbox is checked, the button says "Save Souvenir" and returns the user to the SvnrListView. Otherwise it says "Share Souvenir" , and when tapped, the ContactReviewSheet hides and slides down, and ViewsComponentsShareSheet slides up. If dismissed, the ShareSheet slides down and the ContactReviewSheet slides back up.
  8. The ViewsComponentsShareSheet populates the thumbnail at the top with the image either captured or chosen from the library. When the user taps Send as iMessage, we see the photo populate in iMessage.

## Pending solution

- [ ] The BOOP colors on onboarding are weird and unreadable. Should be a spectrum from danger, warning, winning, success, accent, info, bottom to top.


- [+] Birthday doesn’t import from selected contact?? - Code implemented in ServicesContactSearchService, birthday extracted from system contacts. May be permission/sync issue or user error.
- [-] First contact results shouldn’t hide the Search your Contacts button, they should show up below it - FIXED: Button now always displays above search results in ViewsAddSouvenirView.contactSearchResults. Restructured to use VStack with permission button outside SearchResults conditional.
- [-] Share Souvenir sheet doesn’t show photo just taken: "Please finish adding a photo and primary contact before sharing." even though I did both of those things. With a photo from library I see a perpetual spinner where the thumbnail should be. A fresh capture - FIX: Now obtains permanent IDs before validation (was checking empty array with temp IDs). 3-second timeout added to photo loader to prevent spinner hang.
  - [-] After sharing souvenir, I should return to ListView - Implementation confirmed in AddSouvenirView (navigates to .souvenirList on ShareSheet dismiss)
- [-] Phone number area code is not editable? - Field is editable but aggressive formatting may make it feel non-editable. Needs user testing.
  - tested and can't backspace into area code, this feels broken

### Testing

- [+] Pinching on an unlocked photo should not dismiss the SouvenirView, it should zoom the photo out.
- [+] Following the souvenir.kerry.ink link in a share crashes the browser. And the text talks about the person being shared with in the third person: should say: "What a trip to know you! Here’s a souvenir 😄🏆"
- [+] cycling between souvenirs the mapAnnotations still don’t reliably show up on when the map arrives at the proper location
    - [+] and when they do show up, they don’t always enlarge properly
    - [+] tapping another souvenir’s mapAnnotation somehow jumps across the map to another photo in the same souvenir which I’m guessing is the favoritePhoto for that souvenir
    
### iPad Issues

- [+] Add Souvenir Capsule View is waaay too big on iPad (becomes fullscreen in landscape)
- [+] ListView ought to be two independently scrolling columns on iPad
- [+] ContactIsland is riding high on iPad
- [+] ViewContactSheet is close but need a jumbo of height or so
- [+] map controls can be twice as big on iPad
- [+] on iPad the ContactSearchSheet and other sheets can be 360 pt wide at most
- [+] on iPad toolbar can be twice as tall

---

# Completed Pre-beta issues

- [x] The confirmation button for location search needs to be outside the search field and trailing, like the save button in ReviewContactSheet when editing contact island
- [x] Birthtime belongs in the bio, not in the ViewContactSheet or ReviewContactDetails sheets. Bio can be restyled to look like ViewContactSheet, and needs a .medium detent, not fullSheet
- [x] Hands and heart symbols don’t show in onboarding after the BOOP!
- [x] creating my first souvenir, I see a big opaque ContactSearch sheet; I need it to be translucent like the other sheets - STILL OPAQUE!!!
    - [x] the ContactSearchField is crowded way up at the top of the big opaque sheet, needs .padding(.vertical, .medium) and .padding(.horizontal, .large)
    - [x] I select my contact and the phone number and email do not populate in the ReviewContactSheet
    - [x] the Give Souvenir button is inactive although I have a photo, name, phone, and email complete
    - [x] I get hung up at the Share Souvenir sheet "Please finish adding a photo before sharing"
- [x] landscape SouvenirView contracted ViewContactSheet covers the whole screen
- [x] the contents of the ContactViewSheet should not scroll, on drag or otherwise
- [x] the difference blended souvenirControls are pretty ugly in some situations; we should give these buttons backgrounds
- [x] choosing a photo from the library I often see a weird lag in the transition that looks really broken, halves of photos in boxes flying around. we need to look at the pipelines and see what gets kinked up there, make it smooth as butter
- [x] the ContactSearchField is crowded way up at the top of the big opaque sheet, needs .padding(.vertical, .medium) and .padding(.horizontal, .large)
- [x] I select my contact and the phone number and email do not populate in the ReviewContactSheet
- [x] adding a new photo to an existing souvenir, the carousel should then advance to the position of that photo when it succeeds
- [x] @LocationSearchIsland has awkward extra vertical padding above the search box; should be laid out the same as the @ContactIsland and its search box
- [x] "email@example.com" placeholder shows in blue and should be muted and grayscale
- [x] Unknown Contact needs to clear and say "Name, please"
- [x] gave location access in onboarding first souvenir, no location displays; still says "Add location"; tapping that Add location again shows the location as intended
- [x] the @ContactIsland is not translucent anymore! what changed? something needs to be .clear that isn't anymore but used to be
- [x] the @PhotoMetadataPill ("pill") starts out floating awkwardly above the compact ContactIsland, but then finds its place once the contactIsland is enlarged
- [x] if the user has rejected or granted limited contact access, and they search for another contact in the ContactIsland search box, can we prompt them again to grant access? or link them to the Settings for this?
- [x] tapped Add location on a photo from the library without location metadata, went to the map, searched for a location, map centered and zoomed there, then??? should see an enlarged map annotation with a checkmark button. tapped the pill instead, which still said "Add Location", and instead of adding the location I was looking at on the map, it added my phone's location in my hand. We should all the pill to be the confirmation when setting location for an unplaced photo

## Reports
- Phase 18 beta readiness report (2026-02-05): `docs/reports/2026-02-05-phase18-beta-readiness.md`

# ⏸️ ISSUES ICEBOX (Wait for now)

- [ ] compact contactIsland needs vertical alignment and rhythm
- [ ] Where did you meet? need UX and flow design

## 🎯 Core User Experience

## 🎨 Visual Design

## Landscape views
- [ ] landscape SouvenirView needs layout for souvenirControls, metadataPill, and contactIsland
- [ ] landscape MapView needs love too
- [ ] landscale ListView too

## 📸 Camera & Photos

- [ ] auto-populate birthdays of contacts?

## 🔄 Frecency & Data

## 🗺️ MAPS & LOCATION

- map needs to show the familiar pulsing blue dot at my location

### 🎯 Map Clustering & Performance
- [ ] Test zooming in/out and verify pins cluster appropriately
- [ ] BUG: clustering doesn't seem to be working at any zoom level
- [ ] NOTE: MapKit built-in clustering requires MKAnnotation protocol, current implementation uses simple Annotation
- [ ] tapping a map marker to show the compact souvenir zooms the map to contain at least one other map marker
- [ ] MKAnnotation clustering (deferred - requires UIViewRepresentable)

## 🧪 Testing Scenarios
**Location Management:**

## 🎨 DESIGN & BRANDING

### 🖌️ Visual Identity

## 🌐 WEB & SHARING

### 📤 Sharing Features
- [ ] Messages share with App Store invite link (deferred)
- [ ] AirDrop fails on ShareSheet
- [ ] NOTE: "Birthday in X days" logic is sufficient for MVP but may not be perfect across all leap years.

### 🔗 Deep Linking & Universal Links
- [ ] QUESTION: how will we test share links? can we mock this up locally? or do we need to deploy a web service to souvenir.kerry.ink?
- [ ] Now we need to host the AASA file on your server.
- [ ] Create https://souvenir.kerry.ink/.well-known/apple-app-site-association
- [ ] Replace `TEAMID` with your Apple Developer Team ID. The file must be served with `Content-Type: application/json` and no redirects, here is the JSON:

```json
{
	"applinks": {
		"apps": [],
		"details": [
			{
				"appID": "TEAMID.ink.kerry.souvenir",
				"paths": ["/s/*"]
			}
	  ]
	}
}
```

# ✅ CLOSED ISSUES

## Phase 17 Polish
## 📍 Geolocation
- [x] map allows text search for locations?
- [x] when photo has no location or geocode data, add location opens a fuzzy field that reverse geocodes

- [x] the pill gets covered by the medium sheet and shouldn't
  - Test: Pill floats above sheet in compact/medium; can be covered at large
- [x] trying to Add location to a new souvenir, the map goes to my current user location, I pan the map a bit, then the map jumps to San Francisco and the LocationSearchIsland disappears
- [x] trying to add a searched location, it doesn't stick to the photo
- [x] trying to make a new souvenir with a library photo, I see the photo I took for the earlier souvenir that still hasn't geolocated even though it has permission

## Onboarding and New Souvenirs
- [x] during onboarding in the first time experience, I take a photo and it asks for photo library permission without a primer. we need to wait to ask for photo library permission until the user decides to Create Souvenir.
- [x] a new souvenir, new photo, asks for location permission, granted, tap Add location for MeetPlace, goes to map, no pin/annotation, doesn't zoom to userLocation, go back to souvenir draft, no location; very broken

- [x] On the map, swipe the metadata pill left/right and confirm the map centers on each photo’s location in order.

  There is still a lot of funky oddness occurring when I try to progress through the lists of photos or souvenirs and see their locations on the map. Often the mapAnnotation doesn't show up when the map does find the location. Often a small annotation for a different photo shows up in the center of a larger annotation, sometimes these are photos from the same souvenir, sometimes from different souvenirs. Usually/always one of them is in the wrong location.

## Maps, Annotations and the Metadata Pill
- [x] when we rotate the map, the map compass shows up under our custom map controls, under the user location button
- [x] location/photo context mismatches between metadataPill, map annotation, and SouvenirView (wrong photo/location shown when navigating between views)
- [x] Contacts primer appeared with keyboard still showing, covering the primer
- [x] In NewSouvenirView half‑open, check the ContactSearch row has tight vertical
     padding and xmark/checkmark toggles correctly.
  - [x] tapping the little x in the Contact Search bar confirms instead of clears the field. we need the search bar to have its native clear x. And then outside of it, to the right, we have our confirm button that is a checkmark
  - [x] we need the Search box to match the font size of the nameHeader which is .title in .halfOpen and .fullSheet, and .title2 in .compact; probably .jumbo or .xxtraLarge

- [x] tapping the MapAnnotation to go to the SouvenirView, the ContactIsland should be at the small detent; tapping the metadataPill goes to the map but shows the ContactIsland at the medium detent and should be at the small detent
- [x] can we show the metadataPill on the map?
- [x] moving to the MapView without a Souvenir or Photo in context, the LocationSearchIsland shows at its large detent obscuring the map; should show at medium.

## PhotoCarousel
- [x] removePhoto on ``@ViewsSouvenirView.swift`` doesn't remove the photo
- [x] in ``@ViewsComponentsPhotoCarouselView.swift`` swiping doesn't wrap-around from last to first or first to last photo

## MeetTime
- [x] meetTime gets set initially to the createdAt time of the first photo for a new souvenir
- [x] we display the MeetTime as a friendly relative datetime in the ContactIsland under the MeetPlace; we use the `DateFormatters.compactRelativeString`
  - and we update the MeetTime like the Birthday: tap the MeetTime, date wheels appear, choose a date, hit Done
    - [x] right now we see date and timerea below the name like an accordion drawer

- [x] there is a race condition that lets me select two photos from the library before it advances and uses one for the new souvenir
- [x] taking a new photo, the metadataPill shows "Locating…" but doesn't resolve

## Islands, Sheets and Detents
- [x] sheet medium detent needs to be shorter, maximum 0.3820 of screen height, minimum 0.1618 of screen height, and responsive to its contents
- [x] sheet large detent needs to be shorter, maximum of 0.8382 of screen height
## Editing Name and ContactSearch
- [x] in ContactIsland, after a search result is selected, the search field is hidden and we only see the name with the edit pencil and left arrow next to it
    - the background of the name is ultraThinMaterial, so we can see the contact search field behind it
    - to the right edge of the sliding name row is an arrow pointing left and the edit pencil, both small and no background
    - choosing one of the names in the drawer closes the drawer and the name populates and slides in from the left
- [x] contactsearch results look like rows, so the whole row should be tappable
- [x] can the small sheet still swipe among the souvenirs and jump around the map? the chevrons work for cycling souvenirs, so I think we just need the gestures. we don't need the souvenir cycle sideswipe gesture at medium or large detent
  - Test: compact swipe cycles souvenirs; medium/large do not respond to sideways swipe
- [x] header content should be the same for all sizes of a sheet: location name or contact name
  - [x] small sheet header needs to be .font.title2
  - Test: Compact + medium + large all show the same header text and sizing
- [x] PhotoMetadataPill needs to be outside the ContactIsland sheet; it relates to the photo, not the contact. The pill needs to float above the sheet if possible, in medium and small detents especially. It's okay if the pill gets covered by the sheet at large detent.

### ContactIsland and LocationSearchIsland Sheets
- [x] all sheets need to be ultraThinMaterial and thus translucent
  - Test: ContactIsland, LocationSearchIsland, contacts primer, photos primer, zodiac sheet translucency are all opaque, likely due to their contents having default opaque backgrounds; needs a comprehensive analysis and elimination of default opaque backgrounds to support liquid glass iOS 26 Material UI in a conventional and reliable way
- [x] ContactIsland search: native search bar appears in half‑open/full‑sheet typing triggers results after 3+ chars, results list scrolls.
  - [x] in NewSouvenirView, the ContactSearch field doesn't show up, just the pill, so there is no way to proceed with Souvenir creation.

- [x] Contact selection: picking a result fills the contact, closes search focus, and expands to full‑sheet when expected.
  - [x] the ContactIsland appear in large fullSheet so I can't see the captured photo; the large sheet needs to be bit shorter to maintain context with the photo
  - [x] the large sheet after contact search and selecting a result still shows the search results "No matches"; we need to hide the search results after the search field is blurred and a result is chosen

- Contact fields: phone/email/birthday render via native rows, input works, and
    buttons (call/SMS/email) enable only when valid.
    - [x] fields or their rows don't need backgrounds behind them
    - [x] contact fields need to be viewable in medium sheet: phone, email, meeting place and time, birthday+zodiac sign
        - [x] tapping email or phone expands to full large sheet and focuses that field for editing
- Zodiac
  - [x] zodiac needs to default to medium sheet and not shrink to small
  - [x] gradient background should apply to entire sheet
  - [x] zodiac sheet cannot be dismissed; swipe down to dismiss from medium

- Delete Souvenir: long‑press pencil → Delete Souvenir shows confirmation dialog and deletes on confirm.
  - [x] Delete souvenir button now hides at the bottom of the full sheet and requires confirmation dialog

- LocationSearchIsland: native search bar + list, typing yields results, selecting a result applies location and collapses to half‑open.
  - tapping "Where did you meet?" and choosing a location goes nowhere
  - [x] LocationSearchIsland needs to start in medium so we can see the map, search can happen in medium; expand to large to see more results

- [x] Remove Location: map annotation context menu → Remove Location shows confirmation dialog and clears the location.

- [x] dragging down on a new souvenir seems to dismiss the newSouvenirView (we don't want this anymore)
- [x] choosing a photo from the library, the library pauses for a moment, then disappears to reveal the camera frozen for a second or two, then shows the chosen photo in a souvenir. We need to make this faster, or show a loading indicator and not a frozen camera feed.
- [x] the chosen or taken photo rides to the right instead of centering; perhaps we haven't detected its dimensions yet, but why is it bigger than the screen it was just taken on? can we just fit it to the background if it is a portrait selfie?
- [x] the name at the top of the fullSheet ContactIsland needs to be centered
  - [x] the edit pencil needs to trail off the end of it but not affect its centeredness
  - [x] the name and edit pencil need micro padding between
  - [x] there is too much padding between the name/contactSearch and the Where did you meet fields

## 🚪 Phase 16 BUGS and Polish

## NewSouvenir and Onboarding
- more progressive enhancement for ContactIsland
    - [x] don't show phone email birthday until name entered
    - [x] half-open after photo capture, tap name to expand to full-sheet and contactSearch
    - [x] edit pencil sits to the right of the name

## SvnrMapView
- [x] jumping to focus @``SvnrMapView`` on a souvenir by tapping @``MapAnnotation`` or Location on @``PhotoMetadataPill`` should center annotation horizontally and vertically centered but offset upwards 1/6 of the screen height. the current `latitudeOffset` does not accomplish this. the annotation appears perfectly centered instead

    - [x] ContactIsland souvenir cycling wraps around when swiping or tapping chevrons

  - Already correct: Chevrons flank name, page dots below with Spacing.micro padding

- [x] photo view mode and edit mode
  - [x] tapping the lock doesn't toggle the lock but sometimes does change to edit mode!

  - [x] metadataPill doesn't appear connected to the photoCarousel
    - [x] swiping on metadataPill changes metadataPill but not photo in background

lines: +- [ ] Dragging contacts in the Rings list causes visual flickering; need smooth animation without frame drops

+- [ ] Test drag-to-reorder cascade logic when moving contact between rings (Trunk→Limbs, etc.)
